title: Schefflera hoi
intro: Schefflera hoi, also known as the Taiwanese Schefflera, is a plant species in the family Araliaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and its extracts have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, protecting against cell damage, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Schefflera hoi include lignans, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Schefflera hoi include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Schefflera hoi is classified within the family Araliaceae, commonly known as the ginseng or ivy family. This family consists of about 68 genera and 1,300 species, including several species of Schefflera. Schefflera hoi belongs to the genus Schefflera, which is native to tropical regions of the world. The genus Schefflera is further divided into several sections, with Schefflera hoi being part of one of these sections. Within the family Araliaceae, Schefflera hoi is a distinct species with its unique characteristics and growth habits.
